title: Student Welfare: Crisis Intervention
description: Conroe ISD has procedures in place to identify and support students who may be experiencing a personal crisis, such as a mental health emergency or thoughts of self-harm. The district works to connect these students with appropriate care and resources as quickly as possible. Parents and community members can rely on these guidelines to understand how the school responds when a student's safety or well-being is at serious risk.

timeline_local.summary: Revisions to Board Policy FFB (Local) were discussed. No action was taken.

## Board Policy — Second Reading (Action Items)

### Items A through H (State-Mandated Updates):
Policies: CH Local, CKC Local, CK Local, CL Local, DGC Local, EIA Local, FFAC Local, FFB Local
- Board noted these are state-mandated changes from the legislative session, not district-initiated
- Discussion about future process: putting routine legal updates on consent agenda; Dr. Vinson committed to providing small group review and board brief previews
- Motion included authorization for non-substantive grammatical changes
- **Vote: Approved 7-0**

### Item I: FFG Local — Studen
timeline_local.notes: Listed as a Board Policy Discussion Item. No action was taken. No specific change language available in these pages.

timeline_local.summary: This page is a summary/cover document describing changes to five Local policies, all informed by recent Texas legislation: (1) DGC(Local): Updated per House Bill 6 to outline when professional employees are immune from disciplinary proceedings and to define disciplinary proceedings. (2) EIA(Local): Updated per Senate Bill 12 to add interim report requirements for unsatisfactory performance, require at least two parent-teacher conference opportunities, and mandate parent signature on progress reports at least every 12 weeks. (3) FFAC(Local): Updated per Senate Bill 920 to expand who may authorize a student to receive nonprescription medication (now includes legal guardians) and require dosing consistent with official container labeling. (4) FFB(Local): Updated per House Bills 6 & 121 to specify professionals required on the safe and supportive school team, allow a team to serve more than one campus, clarify when parents must be contacted, and establish new guidelines for threat assessments involving special education students. (5) FFG(Local): Updated per Senate Bill 571 and House Bill 4623 to identify sexual misconduct and improper student–teacher relationships as explicitly reportable, reduce reporting timeline from 48 to 24 hours, add students in educator preparation programs and Board Trustees as mandatory reporters, establish a parent notification process for alleged staff misconduct, and state that individuals may not assert official immunity for failure to report.
changes_added: DGC(Local): Circumstances under which a professional employee is immune from disciplinary proceedings; definition of disciplinary proceedings

EIA(Local): New requirements for interim reports when student performance is unsatisfactory; at least two opportunities for teacher conferences; parent signature on progress reports at least every 12 weeks
FFAC(Local): Legal guardian added as authorized to grant permission for nonprescription medication; dosing must be consistent with container's official labeling

FFB(Local): Requirements regarding types of professionals on safe and supportive school team; conditions under which team may serve more than one campus; team responsibility for parent contact timing; guidelines for threat assessments involving special education students

FFG(Local): Sexual misconduct and improper student–teacher relationships explicitly identified as reportable; 24-hour reporting timeline; students in educator preparation programs and Board Trustees identified as mandatory reporters; new parent notification process for alleged staff misconduct involving their student; explicit statement that individuals may not assert official immunity and may incur legal liability for failure to report

### FFB Local — Student Welfare, Crisis Intervention
- Deals with threat assessment teams per campus (House Bill 6).
- Discussion about whether the policy is overly prescriptive about who must serve on threat assessment teams; law requires one team per campus but may allow more flexibility in membership.
- **Board consensus:** Amend to provide more flexibility in team composition while maintaining one
timeline_local.notes: This is a summary/cover page (page 290, labeled page 3) from a Texans for Excellence in Education (TEE) document, not a redline policy document itself. No underlined or strikethrough text is present; changes are described in narrative form. The action type (first reading, second reading, adoption) is not specified on this page. The meeting date is 2026-01-20.

timeline_local.summary: Policy FFB (Local) is being updated to add requirements related to the multidisciplinary threat assessment and safe and supportive team. Key additions include: (1) ensuring team members have expertise in specific areas, (2) adding parental notification and participation requirements before and after threat assessments, and (3) referencing Education Code 37 in the threat assessment process.
changes_added: The Superintendent of the district shall ensure, to the greatest extent practicable, that the members appointed to each team have expertise in counseling, behavior management, mental health and substance use, classroom instruction, special education, school administration, school safety and security, emergency management, and law enforcement.
and Education Code 37 (added to Threat Assessment Process section)
a. Notifying the parent of or person standing in parental relation to the student regarding the assessment.
b. Providing an opportunity for the parent of or person standing in parental relation to the student to participate in the assessment, either in person or remotely.
c. Providing an opportunity for the parent of or person standing in parental relation to the student to submit to the team information regarding the student.
4. After completing a threat assessment of a student, providing to the parent of or person standing in parental relation to the student the team's findings and conclusions regarding the student.

If the student at risk of suicide also makes a threat of violence to others, the team shall conduct a threat assessment in addition to actions taken in accordance with the district's suicide prevention program.

timeline_local.notes: This is page 1 of 2 of policy FFB (Local) for Conroe ISD. Added text appears in red/underlined formatting. No strikethrough (removed) text is visible on this page. The document is labeled 'Update: 1' and 'Policy Update Page Number 15'.

description_local: This policy governs how the school district responds to crisis situations involving students, including threats to safety, and outlines the teams and procedures put in place to assess and address those situations. It applies to students, school staff, and the broader school community who may be affected by a crisis or safety concern. Parents and community members should be aware of this policy because it directly shapes how the district identifies, evaluates, and responds to potential threats, helping to ensure that schools remain safe and supportive environments for all students.
